intentional_behaviors.claim: sanitize option is opt-in; default-false means the operator is trusted to vet spec content. Setting sanitize:true is the documented mitigation for untrusted specs.
intentional_behaviors.quote: sanitize — If set to true, the API definition is considered untrusted and all HTML/Markdown is sanitized to prevent XSS.

intentional_behaviors.claim: untrustedSpec is the deprecated alias of sanitize; same opt-in semantics.
intentional_behaviors.quote: untrustedSpec — If set to true, the API definition is considered untrusted and all HTML/Markdown is sanitized to prevent XSS.

intentional_behaviors.claim: MDX-style vendor tags <security-definitions>, <security-definition>, <schema-definition> are operator-facing surface meant to be injected into spec description text.
intentional_behaviors.quote: You can inject the Security Definitions widget anywhere in your specification description: <!-- Acme-Inject: <security-definitions> -->

intentional_behaviors.claim: demo/ is the public playground site, not a published library asset; remediation of demo-only issues lies outside the npm package.
intentional_behaviors.quote: demo/** — demo site, not shipped with the library.

intentional_behaviors.claim: marked raw HTML pass-through is by design; default sanitize=off means operator opts in to DOMPurify only when needed.
intentional_behaviors.quote: marked raw HTML — by design; only relevant when options.sanitize=false AND spec is untrusted. Default config is 'sanitize off'; do NOT report unless paired with a use-case where spec is untrusted.

intentional_behaviors.claim: SourceCode and JsonViewer dangerouslySetInnerHTML sinks are gated by Prism HTML-escaping and jsonToHtml htmlEncode respectively; KB declares them review-required, not auto-finding.
intentional_behaviors.quote: dangerouslySetInnerHTML in SourceCode.tsx and JsonViewer.tsx — protected by Prism's internal HTML escaping and by jsonToHtml's htmlEncode; mark as review-required, not auto-finding.

acknowledged_risks.claim: CHANGELOG explicitly records untrusted-spec as the XSS-prevention option — i.e. the project acknowledges that without it, spec-driven XSS is in scope.
KB Threat Model T1/T2/T6/T7/T8 explicitly enumerate spec-driven XSS, href-scheme injection, sanitize-default risk, and reflected-attr risks as in-scope threats.
acknowledged_risks.quote: T1 Spec author injects /
